data.content: Today, President Donald J. Trump agreed to withdraw his March 14, 2025 Executive Order regarding the Paul, Weiss,Â Rifkind, Wharton & GarrisonÂ LLPÂ law firmÂ (âPaul, Weissâ), which has entered into the following agreement with the President:Â 1. Paul, Weiss agrees that the bedrock principle of AmericanÂ Justice is that it must be fair and nonpartisan for all.Â OurÂ JusticeÂ System is betrayed when it is misused to achieve political ends.Lawyers and law firms play a vital role in ensuring that we live up to that standard as a Nation. Law firms should not favor any political party when it comes to choosing their clients.Â Firms alsoÂ shouldÂ notÂ make decisions on whom to hire based on a personâs political affiliation. To do otherwise is to deny some Americans an equal opportunity for our services while favoring others. Â Lawyers abandonÂ theÂ professionâs highest ideals when they engage in partisan decision-making,Â and betrayÂ theÂ ethical obligation to represent those who are unpopular or disfavored in a particularÂ environment. Â 2. Paul, Weiss affirms its unwavering commitment to these core ideals and principles,Â and will not deny representation to clients, including in pro bono matters and in support of non-profits, because of the personal political views of individual lawyers. Â 3. Paul, Weiss will take on a wide range of pro bono matters that represent the full spectrum of political viewpoints of our society, whether âconservativeâ or âliberal.â4. Paul, Weiss affirms its commitment to merit-based hiring, promotion, and retention, and will not adopt, use, or pursue any DEI policies. As part ofÂ itsÂ commitment,Â itÂ will engage experts, to be mutually agreed upon within 14 days, to conduct a comprehensive audit of all ofÂ itsÂ employment practices.5. Paul, Weiss will dedicate theÂ equivalent ofÂ $40 million in pro bono legal services over the course of President Trumpâs term to supportÂ the AdministrationâsÂ initiatives, including: assisting our Nationâs veterans,Â fairness in the Justice System,Â the Presidentâs Task Force to Combat Antisemitism, and other mutually agreed projects.Statement from the White House: âThe President is agreeing to this action in light of a meeting with Paul, Weiss Chairman,Â Brad Karp,Â during which Mr. Karp acknowledged the wrongdoing of former Paul, Weiss partner, Mark Pomerantz, the grave dangers of Weaponization, and the vital need to restore our System of Justice.âIn response to the Presidentâs announcement,Â Paul, Weissâs Chairman BradÂ Karp said:Â âWe are gratified that the President has agreed to withdraw the Executive Order concerning Paul, Weiss. We look forward to an engaged and constructive relationship with the President and his Administration.â
